title: penstemon confusus subsp. typicus
intro: Penstemon confusus subsp. typicus, also known as the typic beardtongue, is a plant species in the family Plantaginaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting respiratory health. Herbal preparations of Penstemon confusus subsp. typicus can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or using them in tinctures and teas.
